summary: Unused dependency on @sql-trigger/nodesql loaded at module top
details: index.js unconditionally requires `@sql-trigger/nodesql` at the top of the main file (`var ins = require('@sql-trigger/nodesql');`) but never references the imported value anywhere else in the package. The package's stated purpose is an npm publish helper exposing `localPackage`, `remoteVersion`, and `publish` via the npm programmatic API; the @sql-trigger-scoped dependency has no functional role in that purpose and belongs to an unrelated scope from a different publisher. The shape — innocuous wrapper code plus a publisher-mismatched, purpose-mismatched dependency that fires on require — is consistent with dependency smuggling, where the visible package serves only to land a sibling-scoped package onto installer machines at module load. The package itself contains no install-time lifecycle hook and no exfiltration code; the Travis-gated `npm.registry.adduser` path reads NPM_USERNAME/PASSWORD/EMAIL only when a consumer explicitly invokes `publish()` under Travis and sends them to the legitimate npm registry, which is the documented behavior of a publish helper and not a concern. The risk hinges on the contents and intent of `@sql-trigger/nodesql`, which must be evaluated separately to determine whether installer harm actually fires.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
